item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image #1 (2556a.jpg)
Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:B47E_in_flight.jpg
Author: U.S. Air Force
Licence: Public Domain

Image #2 (2556b.jpg)
Source: https://www.flickr.com/photos/sdasmarchives/8126107170/
Author: SDASM Archives
Licence: No known copyright restrictions

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brought a tear to my eye. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a 200 square nautical mile radius surrounding SCP-2556

I'm pretty sure that a radius would be measured in nautical miles, not square nautical miles.

You're either looking for an 8 nautical mile radius or a 200 square nautical mile area. Or a 200 nautical mile radius, which would actually make more sense I suppose, I dunno which you're looking for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Short, simple, and sweet. I hope you come back to the site soon, Kate; your SCPs are among my favorite recent additions to the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 'scuse me for a sec, got an alpha particle in my eye… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not often that a scip winds up heartwarming, but a fine piece it turned out to be. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dunno, really. It just does not fit their usual m.o., I think… why going straight nuclear when they have zero way to deliver it anywhere?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Captain Awen could be from a rogue faction.

Also, it's 1956. Even if you don't intend to use it, why wouldn't you want to become a nuclear power?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… ¿el frente de liberación? Wanting to go nuclear?

Seriously?

It doesn't stop me from upvoting, but it's sort of jarring. Not the kind of approach the Polisario has or had back then, as far as I can remember. :-/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is sweet, sad, and makes the Foundation the good guys for once. Floated my upboat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a bit curious what you thought I was going for, to see if you and I actually made that connection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The best way I can think of to compliment this is that I think I understand exactly what you were going for with this SCP, and I think you executed it perfectly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one a lot. The concept was nice, it made me feel sad, and it was unique — I can't even remember the last SCP I've read that was cooperative and helpful towards the foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I used it to refer to URLs on the Foundation database in SCCP-2930, as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just say the updated article but what is this?

⦿/Procedures/2556/addendum1.ftml.

What even is that symbol?

+1 regardless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I changed it to a reference. You're right, expungement was the wrong term.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good but one problem.
(Picture sock puppets in lab coats saying this)

Further scientific details on the nature and theorized sources of SCP-2556’s radiation are available to personnel with appropriate security clearance at [DATA EXPUNGED].

La la la. Im a foundation worker with high level clearence la la la. Geez I sure do need to know details on the nature of the radiation…oh wait…I dont know where to go look for that because the information has been purged from the database….well better go home!

See my point? How would anyone know where to look.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. It's a fairly simple idea for an anomalous object, with a well executed story about the people affected by it.

I prefer to think of the Foundation as cold rather than cruel, and it's refreshing to see humanoid containment that's not aggressively harsh and dehumanizing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know whether it was intentional or not, but thank you for not using the real names of the missing personnel. There's just too many ways that that could have turned out distasteful.

Completely agreed, and that decision is on purpose. The story was inspired by that event but all relevant and personal details are entirely fictional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know whether it was intentional or not, but thank you for not using the real names of the missing personnel. There's just too many ways that that could have turned out distasteful.

That said, the actual records of the flight appear to list the three crew members as "aircraft commander, observer, and pilot", not "pilot, copilot, and crew".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the purpose of this entry was to show that the cold nature of the foundation does not mean abandoning decency and care for others, that purpose is fulfilled and you get my +1. If not, you get my upvote anyway. Good job :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice.
Bonus points for not using the phrase "self-termination" which I am thoroughly allergic to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a student who attends a school specializing in aviation, this skip caught my interest.

The fact that you kept my interest with a solid story earns my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed the typo mentioned and removed that line. I agree, it's unnecessary on-site.

Referring to Honeycutt and Ilowski by their names was a conscious decision, similar to Canastota being named in SCP-2272. I'm going to stick with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's touching. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the story at the end and generally being a good skip, but this needs some cleanup. ("will be automatically report a") The line "For details on the recovery, creation and former crew members of SCP-2556, see sections below." is horrendously unnecessary, and the crew should primarily be referred to by their SCP designations. This is pretty solid, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dem feels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are correcturate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Applicable edit completed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was beautiful +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"It takes patience to appreciate domestic bliss; volatile spirits prefer unhappiness." - George Santayana

New SCP, mostly based on the 1956 B-47 disappearance, with a number of details scrambled. Check out my other works here.
